name: Pfefferneusse
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan over low heat, mix the first four ingredients until blended.
2. Remove from heat and allow to cool. Stir in spices.
3. Stir in egg. Pour into a very large non-metallic bowl.
4. Sift together dry ingredients and add, in fourths, to liquid mixture, mixing well after each addition. (The dough will be VERY stiff.)
5. Cover dough with plastic wrap and allow to stand at room temperature overnight to blend flavors.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F.
7. Shape dough into 1-inch balls and place on an ungreased cookie sheet.
8. Bake about 12 minutes, or until bottoms are lightly browned. Cool momentarily on sheets, then remove to a cooling rack.
9. Shake cookies in powdered sugar while still hot to coat. (I used about 1/2 cup of sugar for the whole batch.)
10. Store in covered containers with a piece of orange or apple peel for several weeks to mellow. (Change the peel every third day or so.) Makes 84-106 cookies, depending on size. (I got 106 this year; last year it was only 98. If you end up with less than 84, the balls of dough are too large and the centers won't bake through.)
This is my grandmother's recipe. Theoretically, this is how these cookies are made in the Baden region of Germany, which is a bit different from other variants of these cookies. I generally let them mellow for 3-4 weeks before eating. Do not store them in tins or glass; they end up with an odd taste. Stoneware or ceramic storage containers seem to work best.
